# Reproduces a legacy allocator bug.
#
# When voter_constraints pin each voter to a specific sibling store on a
# specific node and the existing voter on every node is on the WRONG sibling
# of that node, considerRebalance → RebalanceVoter → RebalanceTarget picks
# a rebalance pair of the form (ADD on n, REMOVE on m) with m != n.
# Concretely, for a range with voters on (n1s1, n2s1, n3s1) and
# voter_constraints = {+node1store2:1, +node2store2:1, +node3store2:1}, the
# planner emits something like (ADD n3,s6 REMOVE n2,s3). The
# allocatorimpl.ValidateReplicationChanges validator then rejects this pair
# with "node 3 already has a replica; only valid actions are a removal or a
# rebalance(add/remove)" because n3 already holds the s5 voter.
#
# Why RebalanceTarget picks a cross-node remove: it simulates "which voter
# to remove after adding s6" with the new voter NOT yet present. From the
# planner's perspective, removing s5 (the would-be same-node sibling)
# leaves the range in a state where the +node3store2 constraint is still
# unsatisfied (because the simultaneous ADD of s6 isn't accounted for), so
# all three existing voters score "constraint check fail" equally and the
# diversity score collapses to 0 across all of them in a single-locality
# cluster. The tie-break then picks an arbitrary cross-node remove.
#
# Without per-store attrs the scenario cannot be expressed in asim, so this
# test depends on set_store_attrs being available. Without the
# allocatorimpl.ValidateReplicationChanges hook in asim's apply path, the
# rejected pair would be applied anyway and the range would converge
# through a transient over-replicated state, hiding the bug.
gen_cluster nodes=3 stores_per_node=2
----

set_store_attrs store=1 attrs=(node1store1)
----

set_store_attrs store=2 attrs=(node1store2)
----

set_store_attrs store=3 attrs=(node2store1)
----

set_store_attrs store=4 attrs=(node2store2)
----

set_store_attrs store=5 attrs=(node3store1)
----

set_store_attrs store=6 attrs=(node3store2)
----

# Many ranges so per-store rangeCount has variance — without that, the
# rebalance scorer's tie-break tends to pick a same-node remove and the bug
# does not surface.
gen_ranges ranges=100 repl_factor=3 placement_type=replica_placement
{s1:*,s3,s5}:1
----
{s1:*,s3,s5}:1

set_span_config
[0,10000): num_replicas=3 num_voters=3 voter_constraints={'+node1store2':1,'+node2store2':1,'+node3store2':1}
----

# Expected to fail: the rejected (ADD on n, REMOVE on m≠n) pair leaves
# many ranges stuck on the wrong-sibling stores. When the underlying
# allocator bug is fixed, this assertion will start passing and the issue
# annotation should be removed.
assertion type=conformance under=0 over=0 unavailable=0 violating=0 issue=https://github.com/cockroachdb/cockroach/issues/170471
----

eval duration=5m cfgs=(sma-count) metrics=(replicas) full=true
----
sma-count:
replicas#1: first: [s1=101, s2=0, s3=101, s4=0, s5=101, s6=0] (stddev=50.50, mean=50.50, sum=303)
replicas#1: last:  [s1=69, s2=32, s3=69, s4=32, s5=76, s6=25] (stddev=21.09, mean=50.50, sum=303)
replicas#1: thrash_pct: [s1=0%, s2=0%, s3=0%, s4=0%, s5=0%, s6=0%]  (sum=0%)
hash: 81fbd893101b4b63
failed assertion sample 1
  conformance unavailable=0 under=0 over=0 violating=0 
  actual unavailable=0 under=0, over=0 violating=76 lease-violating=0 lease-less-preferred=0

==========================
Cluster Set Up
	n1(AU_EAST,AU_EAST_1,8vcpu): {s1:(256GiB),s2:(256GiB)}
	n2(AU_EAST,AU_EAST_1,8vcpu): {s3:(256GiB),s4:(256GiB)}
	n3(AU_EAST,AU_EAST_1,8vcpu): {s5:(256GiB),s6:(256GiB)}
Key Space
	[0,10000): 100(rf=3), 0MiB, [{s1*,s3,s5}:100]
Event
	set s1 attrs=[node1store1]
	set s2 attrs=[node1store2]
	set s3 attrs=[node2store1]
	set s4 attrs=[node2store2]
	set s5 attrs=[node3store1]
	set s6 attrs=[node3store2]
	[0,10000): 3voters [voters:{1:=node1store2}{1:=node2store2}{1:=node3store2}]
	set LBRebalancingMode to 2
Workload Set Up
	empty
Changed Settings
	empty
==========================
